name: autocannon
description: Fast HTTP/1.1 benchmarking tool written in Node.js

name: -c
--connections
options.description: The number of concurrent connections to use

name: -p
--pipelining
options.description: The number of pipelined requests to use

name: -d
--duration
options.description: The number of seconds to run the autocannon

name: -a
--amount
options.description: The number of requests to make before exiting the benchmark

options.name: -L
options.description: The number of milliseconds to elapse between taking samples. This controls the sample interval, & therefore the total number of samples, which affects statistical analyses

name: -S
--socketPath
options.description: A path to a Unix Domain Socket or a Windows Named Pipe. A URL is still required to send the correct Host header and path

name: -w
--workers
options.description: Number of worker threads to use to fire requests

name: -W
--warmup
options.description: Use a warm up interval before starting sampling. This enables startup processes to finish and traffic to normalize before sampling begins

options.name: --on-port
options.description: Start the command listed after -- on the command line. When it starts listening on a port, start sending requests to that port. A URL is still required to send requests to the correct path. The hostname can be omitted, `localhost` will be used by default

name: -m
--method
options.description: The HTTP method to use

name: -t
--timeout
options.description: The number of seconds before timing out and resetting a connection

name: -T
--title
options.description: The title to place in the results for identification

name: -b
--body
options.description: The body of the request

name: -i
--input
options.description: The body of the request

name: -H
--headers
options.description: The request headers

options.name: --har
options.description: When provided, Autocannon will use requests from the HAR file

name: -B
--bailout
options.description: The number of failures before initiating a bailout

name: -M
--maxConnectionRequests
options.description: The max number of requests to make per connection to the server

name: -O
--maxOverallRequests
options.description: The max number of requests to make overall to the server

name: -r
--connectionRate
options.description: The max number of requests to make per second from an individual connection

name: -R
--overallRate
options.description: The max number of requests to make per second from all connections

name: -C
--ignoreCoordinatedOmission
options.description: Ignore the coordinated omission issue when requests should be sent at a fixed rate using 'connectionRate' or 'overallRate'

name: -D
--reconnectRate
options.description: The number of requests to make before resetting a connections connection to the server

name: -n
--no-progress
options.description: Don't render the progress bar. default: false

name: -l
--latency
options.description: Print all the latency data. default: false

name: -I
--idReplacement
options.description: Enable replacement of [<id>] with a randomly generated ID within the request body. default: false

name: -j
--jsonPath
options.description: Print the output as newline delimited JSON. This will cause the progress bar and results not to be rendered

name: -f
--forever
options.description: Run the benchmark forever. Efficiently restarts the benchmark on completion

name: -s
--servername
options.description: Server name for the SNI (Server Name Indication) TLS extension. Defaults to the hostname of the URL when it is not an IP address

name: -x
--excludeErrorStats
options.description: Exclude error statistics (non-2xx HTTP responses) from the final latency and bytes per second averages. default: false

name: -E
--expectBody
options.description: Ensure the body matches this value. If enabled, mismatches count towards bailout. Enabling this option will slow down the load testing

options.name: --renderStatusCodes
options.description: Print status codes and their respective statistics

options.name: --cert
options.description: Path to cert chain in pem format

options.name: --key
options.description: Path to private key for specified cert in pem format

options.name: --ca
options.description: Path to trusted ca certificates for the test. This argument accepts both a single file as well as a list of files

options.name: --debug
options.description: Print connection errors to stderr

name: -v
--version
options.description: Print the version number

name: -V
--verbose
options.description: Print the table with results (default: true)

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help
